userMessage: i want you to go to scope do some measuments actions find me answers you are the enginer in control of the scope

response.text:
Done — the display is an offline TekScope eye-diagram analysis of Ref1. The main plot shows a fairly open eye with vertical rails near ±500 mV and horizontal opening centered around 0 s. The eye readout shows midlevel about 80.36 mV and eye width about 341/4995 to 334/4995 crossings, with the side panel listing EW ≈ 345.9 ps.

The measurement results table shows Meas 1 as Jitter Summary on Ref1 with data rate about 2.5008 Gb/s, 40 samples/UI, TIE sigma about 8.681 ps, total jitter about 54.07 ps, random jitter about 1.680 ps, deterministic jitter about 33.75 ps, DJ-δδ about 30.43 ps, periodic jitter about 1.316 ps, DDJ about 32.44 ps, and DCD about 2.288 ps.

Below left, the TIE spectrum has several discrete spurs rising from a low floor, indicating periodic components. The TIE histogram is centered slightly above 0 ps with a broader positive cluster and a smaller negative tail. The bathtub plot is nearly flat in the visible center region with BER scale markers down to 1E-15.

The waveform view below the eye shows a dense broadband waveform around Ref1 with roughly ±500 to ±600 mV amplitude. On the right, Meas 2 reports period mean about 1.066 ns and Meas 3 reports frequency mean about 1.106 GHz with sigma about 298.4 MHz, min about 359.0 MHz, max about 1.266 GHz, and N = 1874. The setup bar indicates Ref1 at 200 mV/div, 20 GS/s, horizontal 200 ns/div, acquisition single, and offline status.
